functions.name: addCertManager
functions.description: Installs cert-manager into a Kubernetes environment

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name for the cert-manager installation. Each call adds a uniquely-named resource to the application model, so multiple Kubernetes environments must each pass distinct names.

parameters.name: chartVersion
parameters.description: The cert-manager Helm chart version to install. Defaults to a pinned version validated against this Aspire build.

functions.name: addGateway
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes Gateway API Gateway resource to the application model as a child of the specified Kubernetes environment. The gateway generates a `gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 Gateway` resource and one or more `HTTPRoute` resources in the Helm chart output at publish time.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the gateway resource.

functions.name: addHelmChart
functions.description: Adds an external Helm chart to be installed in the Kubernetes environment. The chart is installed via `helm upgrade --install` as a pipeline step after the main application Helm chart is deployed.
functions.remarks:
The chart is installed in a dedicated namespace (defaulting to the chart resource name).
Use `WithNamespace` to override the namespace, and `WithHelmValue`
to set chart values.
By default, `aspire destroy` does not uninstall the external chart, because
it may be shared with workloads outside the Aspire app. Opt in to destroy-time uninstall
by chaining `WithDestroy`.
functions.returns: A resource builder for the Helm chart resource.
functions.signature: addHelmChart(name: string, chartReference: string, chartVersion: string): KubernetesHelmChartResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Helm chart resource (used as release name and namespace if not overridden).

parameters.name: chartReference
parameters.description: The Helm chart reference. Can be an OCI registry URL (e.g., `oci://quay.io/jetstack/charts/cert-manager`) or a chart name from an added repository.

parameters.name: chartVersion
parameters.description: The chart version to install.

functions.name: addIngress
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes Ingress resource to the application model as a child of the specified Kubernetes environment. The ingress generates a `networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress` resource in the Helm chart output at publish time.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the ingress resource. This is used as the Kubernetes resource name.

functions.name: addIssuer
functions.description: Adds a cert-manager `ClusterIssuer` to this cert-manager installation.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name. Also used as the `metadata.name` of the generated `ClusterIssuer`, so it must be a valid DNS-1123 label.

functions.name: addKubernetesEnvironment
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes environment to the application model.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes environment resource.

functions.name: addNodePool
functions.description: Adds a named node pool to the Kubernetes environment.
functions.remarks:
For vanilla Kubernetes, this creates a named reference to an existing node pool.
For managed Kubernetes services (e.g., AKS), the cloud-specific `AddNodePool` overload
provisions the pool with additional configuration such as VM size and autoscaling.
Use `WithNodePool``1` to schedule workloads on the returned node pool.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the node pool. This value is used as the `nodeSelector` value.

functions.name: configureDashboard
functions.description: Configures the dashboard properties for this Kubernetes environment.
functions.remarks:
Use this overload to customize the dashboard container, for example to set a specific host port
or enable forwarded headers for ingress access.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A method that can be used for customizing the dashboard resource.

functions.name: withChartDescription
functions.description: Sets the Helm chart description written to the generated Chart.yaml.

functions.name: withChartName
functions.description: Sets the Helm chart name written to the generated Chart.yaml.

functions.name: withChartVersion
functions.description: Sets the Helm chart version for deployment.

functions.name: withNamespace
functions.description: Sets the target Kubernetes namespace for deployment.

functions.name: withReleaseName
functions.description: Sets the Helm release name for deployment.

functions.name: otlpGrpcEndpoint
functions.description: Gets the OTLP gRPC endpoint for receiving telemetry data.

functions.name: primaryEndpoint
functions.description: Gets the primary HTTP endpoint of the Aspire Dashboard UI.

functions.name: dashboardEnabled
functions.description: Determines whether to include an Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this environment.

functions.name: defaultImagePullPolicy
functions.description: Gets or sets the default policy that determines how Docker images are pulled during deployment. Possible values are: "Always" - Always attempt to pull the image from the registry. "IfNotPresent" - Pull the image only if it is not already present locally. "Never" - Never pull the image, use only the local image. The default value is "IfNotPresent".

functions.name: defaultServiceType
functions.description: Gets or sets the default Kubernetes service type to be used when generating artifacts.
functions.remarks:
The default value is "ClusterIP". This property determines the type of service
(e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer) created in Kubernetes for the application.

functions.name: defaultStorageClassName
functions.description: Specifies the default name of the storage class to be used for persistent volume claims in Kubernetes. This property allows customization of the storage class for specifying storage requirements such as performance, retention policies, and provisioning parameters. If set to null, the default storage class for the cluster will be used.

functions.name: defaultStorageReadWritePolicy
functions.description: Gets or sets the default access policy for reading and writing to the storage.

functions.name: defaultStorageSize
functions.description: Gets or sets the default storage size for persistent volumes.

functions.name: defaultStorageType
functions.description: Specifies the default type of storage used for Kubernetes deployments.
functions.remarks:
This property determines the storage medium used for the application.
Possible values include "emptyDir", "hostPath", "pvc"

functions.name: kubeConfigPath
functions.description: Gets or sets the path to an explicit kubeconfig file for Helm and kubectl commands. When set, all Helm and kubectl commands will use `--kubeconfig` to target this file instead of the default `~/.kube/config`.
functions.remarks:
This is used by Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) integration to isolate credentials
fetched via `az aks get-credentials` from the user's default kubectl context.

functions.name: setDashboardEnabled
functions.description: Determines whether to include an Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this environment.

functions.name: setDefaultImagePullPolicy
functions.description: Gets or sets the default policy that determines how Docker images are pulled during deployment. Possible values are: "Always" - Always attempt to pull the image from the registry. "IfNotPresent" - Pull the image only if it is not already present locally. "Never" - Never pull the image, use only the local image. The default value is "IfNotPresent".

functions.name: setDefaultServiceType
functions.description: Gets or sets the default Kubernetes service type to be used when generating artifacts.
functions.remarks:
The default value is "ClusterIP". This property determines the type of service
(e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer) created in Kubernetes for the application.

functions.name: setDefaultStorageClassName
functions.description: Specifies the default name of the storage class to be used for persistent volume claims in Kubernetes. This property allows customization of the storage class for specifying storage requirements such as performance, retention policies, and provisioning parameters. If set to null, the default storage class for the cluster will be used.

functions.name: setDefaultStorageReadWritePolicy
functions.description: Gets or sets the default access policy for reading and writing to the storage.

functions.name: setDefaultStorageSize
functions.description: Gets or sets the default storage size for persistent volumes.

functions.name: setDefaultStorageType
functions.description: Specifies the default type of storage used for Kubernetes deployments.
functions.remarks:
This property determines the storage medium used for the application.
Possible values include "emptyDir", "hostPath", "pvc"

functions.name: setKubeConfigPath
functions.description: Gets or sets the path to an explicit kubeconfig file for Helm and kubectl commands. When set, all Helm and kubectl commands will use `--kubeconfig` to target this file instead of the default `~/.kube/config`.
functions.remarks:
This is used by Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) integration to isolate credentials
fetched via `az aks get-credentials` from the user's default kubectl context.

functions.name: withAnnotation
functions.description: Adds or updates a Kubernetes annotation on this manifest.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

functions.name: withField
functions.description: Adds or updates a manifest field using a dot-separated path.
functions.remarks:
Polyglot SDKs currently support scalar field values only. Use dot-separated paths to build nested objects with
scalar leaf values; array values are not supported by this method.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The dot-separated manifest field path, for example `spec.replicas` or `data.username`.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The field value.

functions.name: withLabel
functions.description: Adds or updates a Kubernetes label on this manifest.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The label key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The label value.

functions.name: withNamespace
functions.description: Sets the namespace for this manifest.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The Kubernetes namespace.

functions.name: addManifest
functions.description: Adds an arbitrary Kubernetes manifest to this service's generated Helm chart for polyglot callers.
functions.signature: addManifest(apiVersion: string, kind: string, name: string, configure?: (obj: KubernetesManifestResource) => Promise<void>): KubernetesManifestResource

parameters.name: apiVersion
parameters.description: The Kubernetes API version for the manifest.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: The Kubernetes resource kind for the manifest.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Kubernetes metadata name for the manifest.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A callback that configures the manifest fields.

functions.name: parent
functions.description: Gets the Parent property

functions.name: publishAsKubernetesService
functions.description: Publishes the specified resource as a Kubernetes service.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: The configuration action for the Kubernetes service.

functions.name: withAcmeServer
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use a custom ACME directory endpoint (e.g., a private ACME server such as ZeroSSL or step-ca).

parameters.name: serverUrl
parameters.description: The ACME directory URL (e.g., `https://acme.example.com/directory`).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account.

functions.name: withAcmeServerParam
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use a custom ACME directory endpoint with a parameterized email.

parameters.name: serverUrl
parameters.description: The ACME directory URL (e.g., `https://acme.example.com/directory`).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

functions.name: withDashboard
functions.description: Enables the Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this Kubernetes environment.
functions.remarks:
When enabled, an Aspire Dashboard container is deployed alongside the application resources
in the Kubernetes cluster. All resources with OTLP telemetry support are automatically
configured to send telemetry data to the dashboard.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to enable the dashboard. Default is true.

functions.name: withDefaultBackend
functions.description: Sets the default backend for the ingress. The default backend handles requests that do not match any of the defined routing rules.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the default backend service and port.

functions.name: withForwardedHeaders
functions.description: Configures whether forwarded headers processing is enabled for the Aspire dashboard container.
functions.remarks:
This sets the `ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_FORWARDEDHEADERS_ENABLED` environment variable inside the dashboard
container. When enabled, the dashboard will process `X-Forwarded-Host` and `X-Forwarded-Proto`
headers which is required when the dashboard is accessed through a reverse proxy or ingress controller.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: True to enable forwarded headers, false to disable.

functions.name: withGatewayAnnotation
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation to the generated Gateway resource.
functions.remarks:
This sets Kubernetes `metadata.annotations` on the generated K8S Gateway resource,
not Aspire `IResourceAnnotation` instances. These are key-value
string pairs used by ingress controllers for provider-specific configuration.
For Azure Application Gateway for Containers (AGC), you typically need:
`alb.networking.azure.io/alb-name` and `alb.networking.azure.io/alb-namespace`.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

functions.name: withGatewayAnnotationParam
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation with a parameter value that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the annotation value.

functions.name: withGatewayClass
functions.description: Sets the GatewayClass name that selects which controller implementation handles this gateway.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: The GatewayClass name (e.g., `"azure-alb-external"`, `"istio"`).

functions.name: withGatewayClassParam
functions.description: Sets the GatewayClass name using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the GatewayClass name.

functions.name: withGatewayHostnameParam
functions.description: Adds a hostname using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the hostname value.

functions.name: withGatewayHostRoute
functions.description: Adds a host-and-path-based routing rule to the gateway. The rule matches traffic for the specified host and path, routing it to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service. This generates an `HTTPRoute` resource with a `hostnames` filter.
functions.signature: withGatewayHostRoute(host: string, path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: GatewayPathMatchType): KubernetesGatewayResource

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match. Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `PathPrefix`.

functions.name: withGatewayPathRoute
functions.description: Adds a path-based routing rule to the gateway. The rule matches all hosts and routes traffic matching the specified path to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service. This generates an `HTTPRoute` resource attached to the Gateway.
functions.signature: withGatewayPathRoute(path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: GatewayPathMatchType): KubernetesGatewayResource

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `PathPrefix`.

functions.name: withGatewayTlsAuto
functions.description: Configures TLS termination with an auto-generated secret name derived from the gateway name.

functions.name: withGatewayTlsIssuer
functions.description: Adds an HTTPS listener to the gateway and wires it to the supplied cert-manager `ClusterIssuer`. This adds the `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer` annotation to the generated Gateway resource, causing cert-manager to provision and renew a certificate for each gateway listener hostname.
functions.remarks:
Equivalent to calling `WithTls()` followed by
`WithGatewayAnnotation("cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer", issuer.Resource.Name)`,
but type-safe and refactor-friendly. Throws if the gateway and the issuer's
cert-manager installation are not part of the same Kubernetes environment, since
cert-manager is per-cluster and would otherwise silently produce an unsatisfiable
TLS configuration.

parameters.name: issuer
parameters.description: The cert-manager `ClusterIssuer` to issue certificates from.

functions.name: withGatewayTlsParam
functions.description: Configures TLS termination using a parameter for the secret name.

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the secret name.

functions.name: withHelm
functions.description: Configures the Kubernetes environment to deploy using Helm charts.
functions.remarks: Helm is the default deployment engine. Call this method to customize Helm-specific settings.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: An optional callback to configure Helm chart settings such as namespace, release name, and chart version.

functions.name: withHelmChartDestroy
functions.description: Opts the Helm chart in to destroy-time uninstall. When set, `aspire destroy` will run `helm uninstall` for this release as part of the destroy pipeline.
functions.remarks:
External Helm charts are not uninstalled by default because they may be shared
with workloads outside the Aspire app (for example, cert-manager or an ingress
controller installed once for many apps). Opt in only when the chart's lifecycle
is owned by this app.

functions.name: withHelmChartForceConflicts
functions.description: Opts the Helm chart in to `helm upgrade --install --force-conflicts`. When set, Helm's server-side apply forcibly takes over any fields owned by another field manager instead of failing with a conflict.
functions.remarks:
This is most commonly needed for charts whose templates ship admission webhooks
(cert-manager, kyverno, gatekeeper, opa-gatekeeper, etc.) on clusters where another
admission controller — such as the AKS `admissionsenforcer` field manager
installed by the Azure Policy add-on or Deployment Safeguards — mutates the webhook
configuration after install. Helm's Server-Side Apply (used by default for charts
that opt in, including cert-manager) refuses to overwrite fields owned by another
field manager. Without `--force-conflicts`, the next `helm upgrade` fails
with a "conflict with admissionsenforcer" error on the webhook's
`namespaceSelector` (or similar).
See
Deployment Safeguards in AKS
and
Server-Side Apply conflicts
for background.
Unlike the deprecated `--force` / `--force-replace` (which delete and
recreate the resource and are incompatible with Server-Side Apply),
`--force-conflicts` is non-destructive — it only changes which field manager
owns the conflicting field. No resources are deleted or recreated. This flag is
also distinct from Helm's `--take-ownership`, which transfers ownership of an
entire resource between Helm releases and does not address field-level conflicts.
Requires Helm v3.18 or later (the version that introduced `--force-conflicts`
for `helm upgrade --install`). Older Helm versions fail with
`Error: unknown flag: --force-conflicts`.

functions.name: withHelmChartNamespace
functions.description: Sets the Kubernetes namespace for the Helm chart installation. If not set, the namespace defaults to the chart resource name.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The namespace to install the chart into.

functions.name: withHelmChartReleaseName
functions.description: Sets the Helm release name for the chart installation. If not set, the release name defaults to the resource name.

parameters.name: releaseName
parameters.description: The Helm release name.

functions.name: withHelmValue
functions.description: Sets a Helm value for the chart installation. Values are passed to `helm upgrade --install` via `--set` flags.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The value key using dot notation (e.g., `config.enableGatewayAPI`).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to set.

functions.name: withHostname
functions.description: Adds a hostname that this gateway's routes match. Multiple hostnames can be added by calling this method repeatedly. Hostnames are used as `hostnames` in generated `HTTPRoute` resources and as HTTPS listener hostnames when TLS is configured.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

functions.name: withHostname
functions.description: Adds a hostname that this ingress matches. Multiple hostnames can be added by calling this method repeatedly. If no hostnames are configured, the ingress matches all hosts.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

functions.name: withHttp01Solver
functions.description: Adds an HTTP-01 ACME challenge solver to the issuer. cert-manager will satisfy the challenge by provisioning a temporary HTTP route at `/.well-known/acme-challenge/{token}` on the same hostname being validated. This requires the hostname to be publicly reachable on port 80.
functions.remarks:
HTTP-01 is the right choice for gateways exposed via Azure Application Gateway for
Containers (AGC) or any ingress controller that publishes a publicly addressable
hostname. Wildcard certificates require a DNS-01 solver, which is not yet supported.

functions.name: withIngressAnnotation
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation to the generated Ingress resource. These are key-value pairs in the `metadata.annotations` field of the K8S Ingress, commonly used to configure ingress controller-specific behavior.
functions.remarks:
This method sets Kubernetes metadata annotations, not Aspire `IResourceAnnotation`
instances. Use these for controller-specific features like path rewriting, rate limiting,
CORS configuration, or SSL redirect behavior.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key (e.g., `"nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target"`).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

functions.name: withIngressAnnotationParam
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation with a parameter value that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the annotation value.

functions.name: withIngressClass
functions.description: Sets the Kubernetes ingress class name that selects which ingress controller handles this ingress resource.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: The ingress class name (e.g., `"nginx"`, `"traefik"`, `"azure-alb-external"`).

functions.name: withIngressClassParam
functions.description: Sets the Kubernetes ingress class name using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the ingress class name.

functions.name: withIngressHostAndPath
functions.description: Adds a host-scoped path rule to the ingress. The rule matches traffic for the specified host and path, forwarding it to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service.
functions.signature: withIngressHostAndPath(host: string, path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: IngressPathType): KubernetesIngressResource

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `Prefix`.

functions.name: withIngressHostnameParam
functions.description: Adds a hostname using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the hostname value.

functions.name: withIngressPath
functions.description: Adds a path-based rule to the ingress. The rule matches all hosts and forwards traffic matching the specified path to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service.
functions.signature: withIngressPath(path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: IngressPathType): KubernetesIngressResource

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `Prefix`.

functions.name: withIngressTlsAuto
functions.description: Configures TLS termination with an auto-generated secret name derived from the ingress name.

functions.name: withIngressTlsParam
functions.description: Configures TLS termination using a parameter for the secret name.

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the secret name.

functions.name: withLetsEncryptProduction
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt production ACME endpoint.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account. Let's Encrypt uses this address for expiry notifications and rate-limit appeals.

functions.name: withLetsEncryptProductionParam
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt production ACME endpoint, with the contact email supplied via a parameter resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

functions.name: withLetsEncryptStaging
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt staging ACME endpoint. Certificates issued from staging are not trusted by browsers, but the endpoint has much higher rate limits, making it the right choice for development and CI workflows.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account.

functions.name: withLetsEncryptStagingParam
functions.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt staging ACME endpoint, with the contact email supplied via a parameter resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

functions.name: withNodePool
functions.description: Schedules a compute resource's workload on the specified Kubernetes node pool. This translates to a Kubernetes `nodeSelector` in the pod specification targeting the named node pool.

parameters.name: nodePool
parameters.description: The node pool to schedule the workload on.

functions.name: withOtlpServicePort
functions.description: Sets the Kubernetes Service ports for the Aspire Dashboard OTLP endpoints.
functions.remarks:
This sets the `port` field on the generated Kubernetes Service for the OTLP endpoints
while keeping the `targetPort` as the original container port. Application resources
in the cluster send telemetry to these Service ports. Use standard OTLP ports (4317 for gRPC,
4318 for HTTP) if your services are configured with those defaults.

parameters.name: grpcPort
parameters.description: The Service port for the OTLP gRPC endpoint. If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port (18889).

parameters.name: httpPort
parameters.description: The Service port for the OTLP HTTP endpoint. If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port (18890).

functions.name: withProperties
functions.description: Allows setting the properties of a Kubernetes environment resource.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A method that can be used for customizing the `KubernetesEnvironmentResource`.

functions.name: withServicePort
functions.description: Sets the Kubernetes Service port for the Aspire Dashboard HTTP endpoint.
functions.remarks:
This sets the `port` field on the generated Kubernetes Service while keeping the
`targetPort` as the original container port. This is useful when placing the dashboard
behind an ingress controller that expects a specific Service port (for example, port 80).
To access the dashboard from outside the cluster, use `kubectl port-forward` or
configure the environment's `DefaultServiceType`
to `NodePort` or `LoadBalancer`.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: The Service port number. Cluster-internal clients will connect to this port, which routes to the dashboard's container port (18888). If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port.

functions.name: withTls
functions.description: Configures TLS on a Kubernetes Gateway listener

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes `kubernetes.io/tls` Secret.

functions.name: withTls
functions.description: Configures TLS for a Kubernetes Ingress using a K8S secret

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes `kubernetes.io/tls` Secret.

handleTypes.name: CertManagerIssuerResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a cert-manager `ClusterIssuer` resource in the Aspire application model.
handleTypes.remarks:
At deploy time, an issuer is rendered to a `cert-manager.io/v1 ClusterIssuer`
YAML document and applied to the cluster with `kubectl apply` after the
cert-manager Helm chart is installed and its admission webhook is reachable. The
manifest is not baked into the helm chart output; it is applied directly so the
chart and its issuers can be managed and torn down independently.
Cluster-scoped issuers can be referenced by gateways and ingresses across all
namespaces, which matches the typical multi-namespace deployment pattern for
Aspire applications.
Namespace-scoped `Issuer` resources are intentionally not modeled in the initial
release. `AddIssuer` always produces a cluster-scoped
`ClusterIssuer`.

capabilities.name: withAcmeServer
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use a custom ACME directory endpoint (e.g., a private ACME server such as ZeroSSL or step-ca).

parameters.name: serverUrl
parameters.description: The ACME directory URL (e.g., `https://acme.example.com/directory`).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account.

capabilities.name: withAcmeServerParam
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use a custom ACME directory endpoint with a parameterized email.

parameters.name: serverUrl
parameters.description: The ACME directory URL (e.g., `https://acme.example.com/directory`).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

capabilities.name: withHttp01Solver
capabilities.description: Adds an HTTP-01 ACME challenge solver to the issuer. cert-manager will satisfy the challenge by provisioning a temporary HTTP route at `/.well-known/acme-challenge/{token}` on the same hostname being validated. This requires the hostname to be publicly reachable on port 80.
capabilities.remarks:
HTTP-01 is the right choice for gateways exposed via Azure Application Gateway for
Containers (AGC) or any ingress controller that publishes a publicly addressable
hostname. Wildcard certificates require a DNS-01 solver, which is not yet supported.

capabilities.name: withLetsEncryptProduction
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt production ACME endpoint.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account. Let's Encrypt uses this address for expiry notifications and rate-limit appeals.

capabilities.name: withLetsEncryptProductionParam
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt production ACME endpoint, with the contact email supplied via a parameter resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

capabilities.name: withLetsEncryptStaging
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt staging ACME endpoint. Certificates issued from staging are not trusted by browsers, but the endpoint has much higher rate limits, making it the right choice for development and CI workflows.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The contact email registered with the ACME account.

capabilities.name: withLetsEncryptStagingParam
capabilities.description: Configures the issuer to use the Let's Encrypt staging ACME endpoint, with the contact email supplied via a parameter resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder whose value is the contact email registered with the ACME account.

handleTypes.name: CertManagerResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a cert-manager installation on a Kubernetes environment.
handleTypes.remarks:
cert-manager is a Kubernetes add-on that provisions and renews X.509
certificates from sources such as Let's Encrypt. Aspire models cert-manager
as a typed resource so that issuer resources (`CertManagerIssuerResource`)
can be parented to it and gateways/ingresses can reference issuers in a strongly-typed
way via `WithTls(issuer)`.
Under the covers, `AddCertManager` installs cert-manager
using a `KubernetesHelmChartResource` pointed at the upstream
`oci://quay.io/jetstack/charts/cert-manager` chart, with CRDs and Gateway API
support enabled. The chart resource is registered in the model under
`"{name}-chart"` (so the cert-manager wrapper itself can keep the natural
`"{name}"` identifier) and is exposed via `HelmChart` for advanced
configuration.

capabilities.name: addIssuer
capabilities.description: Adds a cert-manager `ClusterIssuer` to this cert-manager installation.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name. Also used as the `metadata.name` of the generated `ClusterIssuer`, so it must be a valid DNS-1123 label.

handleTypes.name: HelmChartOptions
handleTypes.description: Provides options for configuring Helm chart deployment settings on a {@ats-ref type:KubernetesEnvironmentResource}.

capabilities.name: withChartDescription
capabilities.description: Sets the Helm chart description written to the generated Chart.yaml.

capabilities.name: withChartName
capabilities.description: Sets the Helm chart name written to the generated Chart.yaml.

capabilities.name: withChartVersion
capabilities.description: Sets the Helm chart version for deployment.

capabilities.name: withNamespace
capabilities.description: Sets the target Kubernetes namespace for deployment.

capabilities.name: withReleaseName
capabilities.description: Sets the Helm release name for deployment.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesAspireDashboardResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a resource for the Aspire Dashboard deployed into a Kubernetes environment. This resource is used to visualize telemetry data from resources running in the cluster.

capabilities.name: otlpGrpcEndpoint
capabilities.description: Gets the OTLP gRPC endpoint for receiving telemetry data.

capabilities.name: primaryEndpoint
capabilities.description: Gets the primary HTTP endpoint of the Aspire Dashboard UI.

capabilities.name: withForwardedHeaders
capabilities.description: Configures whether forwarded headers processing is enabled for the Aspire dashboard container.
capabilities.remarks:
This sets the `ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_FORWARDEDHEADERS_ENABLED` environment variable inside the dashboard
container. When enabled, the dashboard will process `X-Forwarded-Host` and `X-Forwarded-Proto`
headers which is required when the dashboard is accessed through a reverse proxy or ingress controller.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: True to enable forwarded headers, false to disable.

capabilities.name: withOtlpServicePort
capabilities.description: Sets the Kubernetes Service ports for the Aspire Dashboard OTLP endpoints.
capabilities.remarks:
This sets the `port` field on the generated Kubernetes Service for the OTLP endpoints
while keeping the `targetPort` as the original container port. Application resources
in the cluster send telemetry to these Service ports. Use standard OTLP ports (4317 for gRPC,
4318 for HTTP) if your services are configured with those defaults.

parameters.name: grpcPort
parameters.description: The Service port for the OTLP gRPC endpoint. If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port (18889).

parameters.name: httpPort
parameters.description: The Service port for the OTLP HTTP endpoint. If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port (18890).

capabilities.name: withServicePort
capabilities.description: Sets the Kubernetes Service port for the Aspire Dashboard HTTP endpoint.
capabilities.remarks:
This sets the `port` field on the generated Kubernetes Service while keeping the
`targetPort` as the original container port. This is useful when placing the dashboard
behind an ingress controller that expects a specific Service port (for example, port 80).
To access the dashboard from outside the cluster, use `kubectl port-forward` or
configure the environment's `DefaultServiceType`
to `NodePort` or `LoadBalancer`.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: The Service port number. Cluster-internal clients will connect to this port, which routes to the dashboard's container port (18888). If `null`, the Service port defaults to the container port.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesEnvironmentResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes environment resource that can host application resources.
handleTypes.remarks:
This resource models the Kubernetes publishing environment used by Aspire when generating
Helm charts and other Kubernetes manifests for application resources that will run on a
Kubernetes cluster.

capabilities.name: addCertManager
capabilities.description: Installs cert-manager into a Kubernetes environment

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name for the cert-manager installation. Each call adds a uniquely-named resource to the application model, so multiple Kubernetes environments must each pass distinct names.

parameters.name: chartVersion
parameters.description: The cert-manager Helm chart version to install. Defaults to a pinned version validated against this Aspire build.

capabilities.name: addGateway
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes Gateway API Gateway resource to the application model as a child of the specified Kubernetes environment. The gateway generates a `gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 Gateway` resource and one or more `HTTPRoute` resources in the Helm chart output at publish time.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the gateway resource.

capabilities.name: addHelmChart
capabilities.description: Adds an external Helm chart to be installed in the Kubernetes environment. The chart is installed via `helm upgrade --install` as a pipeline step after the main application Helm chart is deployed.
capabilities.remarks:
The chart is installed in a dedicated namespace (defaulting to the chart resource name).
Use `WithNamespace` to override the namespace, and `WithHelmValue`
to set chart values.
By default, `aspire destroy` does not uninstall the external chart, because
it may be shared with workloads outside the Aspire app. Opt in to destroy-time uninstall
by chaining `WithDestroy`.
capabilities.returns: A resource builder for the Helm chart resource.
capabilities.signature: addHelmChart(name: string, chartReference: string, chartVersion: string): KubernetesHelmChartResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the Helm chart resource (used as release name and namespace if not overridden).

parameters.name: chartReference
parameters.description: The Helm chart reference. Can be an OCI registry URL (e.g., `oci://quay.io/jetstack/charts/cert-manager`) or a chart name from an added repository.

parameters.name: chartVersion
parameters.description: The chart version to install.

capabilities.name: addIngress
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes Ingress resource to the application model as a child of the specified Kubernetes environment. The ingress generates a `networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress` resource in the Helm chart output at publish time.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the ingress resource. This is used as the Kubernetes resource name.

capabilities.name: addNodePool
capabilities.description: Adds a named node pool to the Kubernetes environment.
capabilities.remarks:
For vanilla Kubernetes, this creates a named reference to an existing node pool.
For managed Kubernetes services (e.g., AKS), the cloud-specific `AddNodePool` overload
provisions the pool with additional configuration such as VM size and autoscaling.
Use `WithNodePool``1` to schedule workloads on the returned node pool.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the node pool. This value is used as the `nodeSelector` value.

capabilities.name: configureDashboard
capabilities.description: Configures the dashboard properties for this Kubernetes environment.
capabilities.remarks:
Use this overload to customize the dashboard container, for example to set a specific host port
or enable forwarded headers for ingress access.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A method that can be used for customizing the dashboard resource.

capabilities.name: dashboardEnabled
capabilities.description: Determines whether to include an Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this environment.

capabilities.name: defaultImagePullPolicy
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default policy that determines how Docker images are pulled during deployment. Possible values are: "Always" - Always attempt to pull the image from the registry. "IfNotPresent" - Pull the image only if it is not already present locally. "Never" - Never pull the image, use only the local image. The default value is "IfNotPresent".

capabilities.name: defaultServiceType
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default Kubernetes service type to be used when generating artifacts.
capabilities.remarks:
The default value is "ClusterIP". This property determines the type of service
(e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer) created in Kubernetes for the application.

capabilities.name: defaultStorageClassName
capabilities.description: Specifies the default name of the storage class to be used for persistent volume claims in Kubernetes. This property allows customization of the storage class for specifying storage requirements such as performance, retention policies, and provisioning parameters. If set to null, the default storage class for the cluster will be used.

capabilities.name: defaultStorageReadWritePolicy
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default access policy for reading and writing to the storage.

capabilities.name: defaultStorageSize
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default storage size for persistent volumes.

capabilities.name: defaultStorageType
capabilities.description: Specifies the default type of storage used for Kubernetes deployments.
capabilities.remarks:
This property determines the storage medium used for the application.
Possible values include "emptyDir", "hostPath", "pvc"

capabilities.name: kubeConfigPath
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the path to an explicit kubeconfig file for Helm and kubectl commands. When set, all Helm and kubectl commands will use `--kubeconfig` to target this file instead of the default `~/.kube/config`.
capabilities.remarks:
This is used by Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) integration to isolate credentials
fetched via `az aks get-credentials` from the user's default kubectl context.

capabilities.name: setDashboardEnabled
capabilities.description: Determines whether to include an Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this environment.

capabilities.name: setDefaultImagePullPolicy
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default policy that determines how Docker images are pulled during deployment. Possible values are: "Always" - Always attempt to pull the image from the registry. "IfNotPresent" - Pull the image only if it is not already present locally. "Never" - Never pull the image, use only the local image. The default value is "IfNotPresent".

capabilities.name: setDefaultServiceType
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default Kubernetes service type to be used when generating artifacts.
capabilities.remarks:
The default value is "ClusterIP". This property determines the type of service
(e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer) created in Kubernetes for the application.

capabilities.name: setDefaultStorageClassName
capabilities.description: Specifies the default name of the storage class to be used for persistent volume claims in Kubernetes. This property allows customization of the storage class for specifying storage requirements such as performance, retention policies, and provisioning parameters. If set to null, the default storage class for the cluster will be used.

capabilities.name: setDefaultStorageReadWritePolicy
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default access policy for reading and writing to the storage.

capabilities.name: setDefaultStorageSize
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the default storage size for persistent volumes.

capabilities.name: setDefaultStorageType
capabilities.description: Specifies the default type of storage used for Kubernetes deployments.
capabilities.remarks:
This property determines the storage medium used for the application.
Possible values include "emptyDir", "hostPath", "pvc"

capabilities.name: setKubeConfigPath
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the path to an explicit kubeconfig file for Helm and kubectl commands. When set, all Helm and kubectl commands will use `--kubeconfig` to target this file instead of the default `~/.kube/config`.
capabilities.remarks:
This is used by Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) integration to isolate credentials
fetched via `az aks get-credentials` from the user's default kubectl context.

capabilities.name: withDashboard
capabilities.description: Enables the Aspire dashboard for telemetry visualization in this Kubernetes environment.
capabilities.remarks:
When enabled, an Aspire Dashboard container is deployed alongside the application resources
in the Kubernetes cluster. All resources with OTLP telemetry support are automatically
configured to send telemetry data to the dashboard.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to enable the dashboard. Default is true.

capabilities.name: withHelm
capabilities.description: Configures the Kubernetes environment to deploy using Helm charts.
capabilities.remarks: Helm is the default deployment engine. Call this method to customize Helm-specific settings.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: An optional callback to configure Helm chart settings such as namespace, release name, and chart version.

capabilities.name: withProperties
capabilities.description: Allows setting the properties of a Kubernetes environment resource.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A method that can be used for customizing the `KubernetesEnvironmentResource`.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesGatewayResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes Gateway API Gateway as a first-class resource in the Aspire application model. A Gateway defines listeners (ports, protocols, TLS) and HTTPRoutes attach to it for routing.

capabilities.name: withGatewayAnnotation
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation to the generated Gateway resource.
capabilities.remarks:
This sets Kubernetes `metadata.annotations` on the generated K8S Gateway resource,
not Aspire `IResourceAnnotation` instances. These are key-value
string pairs used by ingress controllers for provider-specific configuration.
For Azure Application Gateway for Containers (AGC), you typically need:
`alb.networking.azure.io/alb-name` and `alb.networking.azure.io/alb-namespace`.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

capabilities.name: withGatewayAnnotationParam
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation with a parameter value that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the annotation value.

capabilities.name: withGatewayClass
capabilities.description: Sets the GatewayClass name that selects which controller implementation handles this gateway.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: The GatewayClass name (e.g., `"azure-alb-external"`, `"istio"`).

capabilities.name: withGatewayClassParam
capabilities.description: Sets the GatewayClass name using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the GatewayClass name.

capabilities.name: withGatewayHostnameParam
capabilities.description: Adds a hostname using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the hostname value.

capabilities.name: withGatewayHostRoute
capabilities.description: Adds a host-and-path-based routing rule to the gateway. The rule matches traffic for the specified host and path, routing it to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service. This generates an `HTTPRoute` resource with a `hostnames` filter.
capabilities.signature: withGatewayHostRoute(host: string, path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: GatewayPathMatchType): KubernetesGatewayResource

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match. Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `PathPrefix`.

capabilities.name: withGatewayPathRoute
capabilities.description: Adds a path-based routing rule to the gateway. The rule matches all hosts and routes traffic matching the specified path to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service. This generates an `HTTPRoute` resource attached to the Gateway.
capabilities.signature: withGatewayPathRoute(path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: GatewayPathMatchType): KubernetesGatewayResource

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `PathPrefix`.

capabilities.name: withGatewayTlsAuto
capabilities.description: Configures TLS termination with an auto-generated secret name derived from the gateway name.

capabilities.name: withGatewayTlsIssuer
capabilities.description: Adds an HTTPS listener to the gateway and wires it to the supplied cert-manager `ClusterIssuer`. This adds the `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer` annotation to the generated Gateway resource, causing cert-manager to provision and renew a certificate for each gateway listener hostname.
capabilities.remarks:
Equivalent to calling `WithTls()` followed by
`WithGatewayAnnotation("cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer", issuer.Resource.Name)`,
but type-safe and refactor-friendly. Throws if the gateway and the issuer's
cert-manager installation are not part of the same Kubernetes environment, since
cert-manager is per-cluster and would otherwise silently produce an unsatisfiable
TLS configuration.

parameters.name: issuer
parameters.description: The cert-manager `ClusterIssuer` to issue certificates from.

capabilities.name: withGatewayTlsParam
capabilities.description: Configures TLS termination using a parameter for the secret name.

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the secret name.

capabilities.name: withHostname
capabilities.description: Adds a hostname that this gateway's routes match. Multiple hostnames can be added by calling this method repeatedly. Hostnames are used as `hostnames` in generated `HTTPRoute` resources and as HTTPS listener hostnames when TLS is configured.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

capabilities.name: withTls
capabilities.description: Configures TLS on a Kubernetes Gateway listener

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes `kubernetes.io/tls` Secret.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesHelmChartResource
handleTypes.description: Represents an external Helm chart to be installed into a Kubernetes environment. This resource models a Helm chart from a repository (OCI or HTTP) that will be installed as a pipeline step after the main application Helm chart is deployed.

capabilities.name: withHelmChartDestroy
capabilities.description: Opts the Helm chart in to destroy-time uninstall. When set, `aspire destroy` will run `helm uninstall` for this release as part of the destroy pipeline.
capabilities.remarks:
External Helm charts are not uninstalled by default because they may be shared
with workloads outside the Aspire app (for example, cert-manager or an ingress
controller installed once for many apps). Opt in only when the chart's lifecycle
is owned by this app.

capabilities.name: withHelmChartForceConflicts
capabilities.description: Opts the Helm chart in to `helm upgrade --install --force-conflicts`. When set, Helm's server-side apply forcibly takes over any fields owned by another field manager instead of failing with a conflict.
capabilities.remarks:
This is most commonly needed for charts whose templates ship admission webhooks
(cert-manager, kyverno, gatekeeper, opa-gatekeeper, etc.) on clusters where another
admission controller — such as the AKS `admissionsenforcer` field manager
installed by the Azure Policy add-on or Deployment Safeguards — mutates the webhook
configuration after install. Helm's Server-Side Apply (used by default for charts
that opt in, including cert-manager) refuses to overwrite fields owned by another
field manager. Without `--force-conflicts`, the next `helm upgrade` fails
with a "conflict with admissionsenforcer" error on the webhook's
`namespaceSelector` (or similar).
See
Deployment Safeguards in AKS
and
Server-Side Apply conflicts
for background.
Unlike the deprecated `--force` / `--force-replace` (which delete and
recreate the resource and are incompatible with Server-Side Apply),
`--force-conflicts` is non-destructive — it only changes which field manager
owns the conflicting field. No resources are deleted or recreated. This flag is
also distinct from Helm's `--take-ownership`, which transfers ownership of an
entire resource between Helm releases and does not address field-level conflicts.
Requires Helm v3.18 or later (the version that introduced `--force-conflicts`
for `helm upgrade --install`). Older Helm versions fail with
`Error: unknown flag: --force-conflicts`.

capabilities.name: withHelmChartNamespace
capabilities.description: Sets the Kubernetes namespace for the Helm chart installation. If not set, the namespace defaults to the chart resource name.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The namespace to install the chart into.

capabilities.name: withHelmChartReleaseName
capabilities.description: Sets the Helm release name for the chart installation. If not set, the release name defaults to the resource name.

parameters.name: releaseName
parameters.description: The Helm release name.

capabilities.name: withHelmValue
capabilities.description: Sets a Helm value for the chart installation. Values are passed to `helm upgrade --install` via `--set` flags.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The value key using dot notation (e.g., `config.enableGatewayAPI`).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to set.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesIngressResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes Ingress as a first-class resource in the Aspire application model. An Ingress defines HTTP routing rules that direct external traffic to services in the cluster.

capabilities.name: withDefaultBackend
capabilities.description: Sets the default backend for the ingress. The default backend handles requests that do not match any of the defined routing rules.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the default backend service and port.

capabilities.name: withHostname
capabilities.description: Adds a hostname that this ingress matches. Multiple hostnames can be added by calling this method repeatedly. If no hostnames are configured, the ingress matches all hosts.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

capabilities.name: withIngressAnnotation
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation to the generated Ingress resource. These are key-value pairs in the `metadata.annotations` field of the K8S Ingress, commonly used to configure ingress controller-specific behavior.
capabilities.remarks:
This method sets Kubernetes metadata annotations, not Aspire `IResourceAnnotation`
instances. Use these for controller-specific features like path rewriting, rate limiting,
CORS configuration, or SSL redirect behavior.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key (e.g., `"nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target"`).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

capabilities.name: withIngressAnnotationParam
capabilities.description: Adds a Kubernetes metadata annotation with a parameter value that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the annotation value.

capabilities.name: withIngressClass
capabilities.description: Sets the Kubernetes ingress class name that selects which ingress controller handles this ingress resource.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: The ingress class name (e.g., `"nginx"`, `"traefik"`, `"azure-alb-external"`).

capabilities.name: withIngressClassParam
capabilities.description: Sets the Kubernetes ingress class name using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: className
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the ingress class name.

capabilities.name: withIngressHostAndPath
capabilities.description: Adds a host-scoped path rule to the ingress. The rule matches traffic for the specified host and path, forwarding it to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service.
capabilities.signature: withIngressHostAndPath(host: string, path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: IngressPathType): KubernetesIngressResource

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname to match (e.g., `"api.example.com"`).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `Prefix`.

capabilities.name: withIngressHostnameParam
capabilities.description: Adds a hostname using a parameter that will be resolved at deploy time.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the hostname value.

capabilities.name: withIngressPath
capabilities.description: Adds a path-based rule to the ingress. The rule matches all hosts and forwards traffic matching the specified path to the given endpoint's backing Kubernetes service.
capabilities.signature: withIngressPath(path: string, endpoint: EndpointReference, pathType?: IngressPathType): KubernetesIngressResource

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The URL path to match (e.g., `"/"` or `"/api"`). Must start with `/`.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The endpoint reference identifying the target service and port.

parameters.name: pathType
parameters.description: The path matching strategy. Defaults to `Prefix`.

capabilities.name: withIngressTlsAuto
capabilities.description: Configures TLS termination with an auto-generated secret name derived from the ingress name.

capabilities.name: withIngressTlsParam
capabilities.description: Configures TLS termination using a parameter for the secret name.

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: A parameter resource builder for the secret name.

capabilities.name: withTls
capabilities.description: Configures TLS for a Kubernetes Ingress using a K8S secret

parameters.name: secretName
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes `kubernetes.io/tls` Secret.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesManifestResource
handleTypes.description: Represents an arbitrary Kubernetes manifest that is emitted with a Kubernetes service.

capabilities.name: withAnnotation
capabilities.description: Adds or updates a Kubernetes annotation on this manifest.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The annotation key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The annotation value.

capabilities.name: withField
capabilities.description: Adds or updates a manifest field using a dot-separated path.
capabilities.remarks:
Polyglot SDKs currently support scalar field values only. Use dot-separated paths to build nested objects with
scalar leaf values; array values are not supported by this method.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The dot-separated manifest field path, for example `spec.replicas` or `data.username`.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The field value.

capabilities.name: withLabel
capabilities.description: Adds or updates a Kubernetes label on this manifest.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The label key.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The label value.

capabilities.name: withNamespace
capabilities.description: Sets the namespace for this manifest.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The Kubernetes namespace.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesNodePoolResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes node pool as a child resource of a {@ats-ref type:KubernetesEnvironmentResource}. Node pools can be referenced by compute resources to schedule workloads on specific node pools using {@ats-ref method:KubernetesEnvironmentExtensions.withNodePool}.

handleTypes.name: KubernetesResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a compute resource for Kubernetes.

capabilities.name: addManifest
capabilities.description: Adds an arbitrary Kubernetes manifest to this service's generated Helm chart for polyglot callers.
capabilities.signature: addManifest(apiVersion: string, kind: string, name: string, configure?: (obj: KubernetesManifestResource) => Promise<void>): KubernetesManifestResource

parameters.name: apiVersion
parameters.description: The Kubernetes API version for the manifest.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: The Kubernetes resource kind for the manifest.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Kubernetes metadata name for the manifest.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: A callback that configures the manifest fields.

capabilities.name: parent
capabilities.description: Gets the Parent property

enumTypes.name: GatewayPathMatchType
enumTypes.description: Specifies the type of path matching used in a Kubernetes Gateway API `HTTPRoute` rule. The values map directly to the `matches[].path.type` field defined by the Gateway API (see ) and are distinct from the Ingress-flavoured `IngressPathType` because the two specs use different vocabularies (Ingress: `Prefix`, Gateway: `PathPrefix`).

memberDocs.name: PathPrefix
memberDocs.description: Matches based on a URL path prefix split by `/`. Equivalent to the Gateway API `PathPrefix` match type.

memberDocs.name: Exact
memberDocs.description: Matches the URL path exactly and with case sensitivity.

memberDocs.name: RegularExpression
memberDocs.description: Matches the URL path against an implementation-defined regular expression. Support is optional in the Gateway API spec; check your controller's documentation before using it.

enumTypes.name: IngressPathType
enumTypes.description: Specifies the type of path matching used in a Kubernetes Ingress rule.

memberDocs.name: Prefix
memberDocs.description: Matches based on a URL path prefix split by `/`. Matching is case-sensitive and done element-by-element. For example, `/api` matches `/api`, `/api/`, and `/api/v1` but not `/apiv1`.

memberDocs.name: Exact
memberDocs.description: Matches the URL path exactly and with case sensitivity.

memberDocs.name: ImplementationSpecific
memberDocs.description: Matching is delegated to the ingress controller. Check the controller's documentation for the supported matching semantics.
